# Principles of Pro Learning

**Principle 1: Focus Is Trainable. The Pomodoro Technique builds focus muscle.**

**Principle 2: Memory Requires Effort. Active recall is the most efficient study method.**

**Principle 3: Sleep Is Learning. The brain consolidates memories during sleep.**

**Case: The Two-Minute Rule. Start with just two minutes. Momentum carries you.**

**Case: The Metaphor. Connect new ideas to familiar ones using analogies.**

**Case: The Error. Mistakes are data. They guide your next steps.**

**Case: The Growth Mindset. Believing you can learn improves your ability to learn.**
**Case: The Curiosity. Curiosity drives deep learning. Follow your questions.**
**Case: The Environment. Your study environment affects your focus. Control it.**
**Case: The Breaks. Taking breaks is not wasting time. Breaks help the brain consolidate.**
**Case: The Fresh Air. Exercise improves brain function and learning capacity.**
**Case: The Hydration. Dehydration impairs cognitive function. Drink water while studying.**
**Case: The Routine. A consistent study routine trains your brain to switch into learning mode.**
**Case: The Planning. Plan what you will study before you start. This reduces decision fatigue.**
**Case: The Review. Daily review of previous material strengthens memory.**
